items.title: Parisians can swim in the River Seine again | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Many European countries have recently experienced heatwaves, with some areas reaching record-breaking temperatures. However, Parisians can now cool off in the River Seine after a century-long ban was lifted. On Saturday, the French capital's iconic waterway opened for swimming for the first time since 1923.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Dozens dead after flash floods - Did the early warning systems fail? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Officials say that at least 27 people have been killed by severe flash flooding in the US state of Texas. Rescue teams are searching for more than 20 girls from a summer camp who are still unaccounted for.
The floods swept through south-central Texas, hitting Kerr County, which lies on the Guadalupe River. Residents were caught off guard when a month's worth of rain fell in just a few hours.

Chapters:
0:00 At least 27 dead, many missing in Texas flash flooding
0:25 John Downey, News anchor at KTRH radio
1:39 Jess Neumann, Associate Prof. of Hydrology at the University of Reading

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Evidence of needle attacks at French festival hard to find | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>About 145 people, mostly young women, reported they were pricked with syringes during Fete de la Musique, a music festival across France. Now a French newspaper reports that there's no evidence for the spike attacks and experts say it's hard to prove. But people on social media have recounted how they experienced it.

items.title: Dry wind threatens to bring new wildfires to Crete | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Authorities in Greece say a massive wildfire on the island of Crete is mostly contained. The blaze broke out on Wednesday and quickly spread to the island's southeastern coast. Officials say some 5,000 residents and tourists were forced to evacuate their homes. But with dry windy weather conditions continuing, the threat may not be over yet. DW's Sofia Kleftaki reports from Heraklion.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Hamas ready to start ceasefire talks 'immediately' as Trump, Netanyahu set to meet | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hamas says it's ready to begin talks immediately on a US-backed proposal for a 60-day ceasefire in Gaza. The plan would involve a phased release of hostages and a significant increase in humanitarian aid to the territory. 
US President Donald Trump said he's optimistic and that there could be a deal next week. He's been pushing both sides for an end to the war. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is due to travel to Washington for talks on Monday.

00:00 Hamas agrees to ceasefire
00:30 DW speaks with journalist Karim El-Gawhary in Cairo

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Ukraine braces for Russian offensive near Sumy amid drone attacks on cities | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russian drone attacks have struck several parts of Ukraine overnight, including residential buildings in the southern region of Kherson. This comes as crews are still clearing debris from drone and missile attacks that battered the capital Kyiv in the biggest aerial bombardment since the start of the war. In his nightly video address, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he and US President Donald Trump had agreed  to work on increasing Kyiv's air defenses. On Friday, Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One about Ukraine, including the potential of sending more Patriot missile systems.
Meanwhile, Russian forces are closing in on Ukraine's Sumy region near Russia's border. And Ukraine is trying to hold them back. Defense lines are being strengthened as tens of thousands of Russian troops advance towards the strategic city.

00:00 Russian drones strike Ukraine overnight
01:00 DW speaks with Frank Ledwidge, former UK military intelligence officer and current senior lecturer in strategic studies at Portsmouth University
04:47 Ukraine braces for Russian offensive in Sumy

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: China or Tibetans: how will the next Dalai Lama be chosen? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>China has condemned the Dalai Lama ahead of his 90th birthday for asserting that Tibetans in exile will choose his successor. Beijing, which labels him a "separatist," insists it will decide who the next Dalai Lama is. Tibetan Buddhists believe the Dalai Lama is reincarnated and identified through spiritual practices. China, however, plans to appoint the next one itself. The Chinese Communist Party has tightly controlled Tibet since its 1950 takeover, leading to the Dalai Lama’s exile in 1959 and ongoing repression of Tibetan Buddhism. Surveillance, military buildup, and bans on his image aim to suppress dissent. This is the latest episode of DW’s explainer series Inside Asian Conflicts. 

#InsideAsianConflicts #tibet #dalailama

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: French lawmakers aim to curb the growing fast fashion market | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Twelve million duty-free parcels arrive in the EU from China every day. Proof of the rapid growth in online fast fashion traders. Now, France is planning a law to challenge disposable clothing. Fast fashion is coming under growing criticism – and not just on the environmental front.

items.title: What could be behind the intensified attacks? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has spoken with Donald Trump about strengthening Ukraine's air defenses. 
Hundreds of Russian drones and missiles have battered the capital Kyiv in the biggest aerial bombardment since the start of the war. Russia has been stepping up its attacks in recent weeks, as the US has halted supplies of some defensive equipment to Ukraine.

items.title: How Trump bill could cede US global market shares to China | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump has signed his "Big Beautiful Bill" into law, a day after it was passed by the US Congress with overwhelming Republican support.
The president formally approved the legislation at the White House during the 4th of July  celebrations. The bill enacts key parts of Trump's agenda including cutting taxes, and boosting spending on defense and immigration control. Democrats and some Republicans criticized sweeping cuts to federal safety net programs, warning millions could lose health coverage. 
Trump defended the bill - one of the most significant victories so far of his second term.


00:00 Trump bill boosts fossil fuel
01:06 Tyson Barker, Political analyst
09:07 Protesters share concerns over Trump bill 
11:40 Liz Schuelke, Policy analyst

#us #trump #economy

items.title: What's behind Russia's recognition of the Taliban government? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russia has become the first country to officially recognize Afghanistan's Taliban government as the country's legitimate authority. This marks a significant step towards international acceptance of the Taliban nearly four years after they took back control of Afghanistan.
